pages.title: Course kit/ Exercises guide
pages.markdown:
### Course kit/ Exercises guide

For support related to the lab environment, please go to ServiceNow for Lab Support. Obtain the Course Code and Reference ID (if available) from the Course Lab Kit. Provide the information requested including TSO ID (if available) as well as a complete description of the problem.

A​ttached is the lab guide necessary to complete the exercises in this course. Download it and save it away to somewhere within easy reach. Each exercise is numbered. Only do the exercise number outlined in the course.

IMPORTANT NOTE: Jeff mentions that you get your UserID and Password from the Course Kit. Here's how that actually looks. Once you log into SkyTap, you'll see this:

The Course Kit contains your logon credentials for the Skytap virtual machine. In addition, the Course Kit identifies your z/OS system and TSO credentials. Enter the PCOMM session for your z/OS system and logon to TSO using the credentials provided. Because we update our system, the SkyTap part of the course might look a little different, but follow the basic instructions, and you'll be on your way!

### 

You won’t see this until you go to “Exercise 1.”

Download the guide to the labs by clicking "Exercise guide":

#### Exercise guide

Continue to the next lesson by marking this one complete. You’ll go through a checklist, and then you’ll be ready to go!

pages.title: Checklist before starting labs
pages.markdown:
### Checklist before starting labs

The link to the Course Lab Kit should be visible once provisioning completes for your lab environment which takes a few minutes. If you do not see the link to the Course Lab Kit after provisioning completes, please refresh the page in your browser.Y​ou will need an IBM ID. If you do not already have one, you’ll need to create one (it’s free!) before logging in.

P​lease run though the checklist to make sure you're ready for the labs:

- By default, your access to these labs will be available for 15 days or a total of 80 hours (whichever comes first). We limit it to that timeline to control our server resources. If your lab access expires you will be provided with a new lab environment the next time you go into the labs. The Course Kit for this new environment will include new TSO credentials. Your work in the expired lab will be lost. To ask for an extension when you get close to the limit, please go to ServiceNow for Lab Support. Obtain the Course Code and Skytap Reference ID. The Course Code and Reference ID are shown at the top left corner of the lab access page (immediately above the link to the Course Lab Kit). Provide the information requested including TSO ID (if available) as well as a complete description of the problem. Extension requests are reviewed on an individual basis.

- Check to see Skytap is up and operational. You should see all green: https://status.skytap.com

- I​n rare instances company firewalls might block access to Skytap. Please check your connection for access and connectivity speeds: https://labs.edu.ihost.com/connectivity_test NOTE: The Skytap region is assigned based on the time zone of the requestor and will ONLY be one of the following: APAC-2 / EMEA / US-Central / AUS-Sydney

- M​ake sure you downloaded the Course Exercise Guide in the previous section and have that open to the first lab. Only do the lab numbers indicated within the course.

- B​e prepared to click on the 'Course Lab Kit' link when you launch Skytap in the next section. That will contain your server name, username, and password for the session. Refer to the course exercise guide for the screen saver password.

Remember that the Enter key in your PCOMM session is initially configured as the right CTRL key. Jeff describes how to change this mapping, if desired, in the System Familiarization video.

Y​ou're ready to go!

pages.title: Exercise 1: Access your lab environment
pages.markdown:
### Exercise 1: Access your lab environment

You will use the IBM Remote Lab Platform (IRLP) to complete your lab session

Instructions

:

1. Ensure that you have downloaded the relevant exercise guide.

2. Access the lab using the 'Open Lab' button shown here.

3. Log in with your IBM ID (

[Sign up here](https://www.ibm.biz/sign-up-ibm-id)

if you don't already have one), complete the labs by following the exercise guide and return to this course.

Note that labs are not automatically marked complete with a green tick.

This is not a problem and won't prevent you from completing the course and obtaining the badge.

#### Open Lab

pages.title: z/OS 2.3 ISPF User Guide
pages.markdown:
### z/OS 2.3 ISPF User Guide

This document contains two PDFs. The first part provides introductory information about using ISPF:

• An overview of ISPF and the ISPF user interface

• How to enter commands and perform other actions

• How to use libraries and data sets

The second part contains the appendixes. These cover installing the ISPF Client/Server component, using the ISRDDN diagnostic utility, and other reference material such as sample listings and command abbreviations.

This document is for application programmers who use ISPF. Users should be familiar with coding CLISTs, REXX EXECs, or programs in the MVS environment.

I​t's intended as reference only, but do take some time to review these manuals for better familiarization with ISPF.

ISPF User's Guide Volume I

ISPF User's Guide Volume II
